overview.tagline: A proud borough with deep Polish-American roots and a strong sense of community
overview.description: The Borough of Wallington is a compact, close-knit community in southeastern Bergen County, long celebrated for its vibrant Polish-American heritage. The borough features a walkable downtown along Paterson Avenue with Polish bakeries, delis, and restaurants alongside diverse businesses reflecting its evolving character. Wallington's affordable housing, central location, and strong community organizations make it an attractive choice for families and first-time homebuyers.
highlights: Rich Polish-American heritage with authentic bakeries, delis, and cultural events
Affordable housing market relative to Bergen County
Strong community organizations and volunteer spirit
Central location with easy access to major highways

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Wallington's housing stock is predominantly single-family homes and two-family properties on modest lots.
realEstate.recentTrends: Wallington has seen significant price appreciation as buyers priced out of more expensive Bergen County towns discover its affordability and central location. Two-family homes are particularly popular as investment properties. The market is competitive with limited inventory.
neighborhoodNotes: Paterson Avenue area — Walking distance to shops, bakeries, and bus service
Main Avenue section — Residential neighborhood with well-kept homes
Locust Avenue area — Quiet residential streets near the Passaic River
East Wallington — Near the Saddle Brook border with easy highway access
North end — Residential area near the Garfield border

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of Wallington.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment through DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected weekly for all residential properties.
details: Commingled recyclables collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on recycling day
Yard waste collected seasonally
Recycling bins available from the borough
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is provided by Veolia Water and sewer service is maintained by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water / Suez
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Quarterly billing for water and sewer
Water quality reports published annually
Emergency water repairs available 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and changes of use

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Street parking is available throughout the borough with designated regulations.
details: Street parking available on most residential streets
Parking meters or time limits in the Paterson Avenue business district
Overnight parking restrictions enforced on some streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Municipal lot available near Borough Hall

additionalServices.title: John F. Kennedy Memorial Library
additionalServices.description: Public library serving the Wallington community with books, programs, and resources.
details: Located at 92 Hathaway Street
Children's programs and adult activities
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Free Wi-Fi and computer access

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Wallington enforces noise regulations to maintain residential quality of life.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 8:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Loud music and amplified sound restricted after 10:00 PM
Commercial operations must comply with noise limits near residential areas

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Properties must be maintained in neat and clean condition
Sidewalk snow removal required within 24 hours after snowfall
Fences must comply with height regulations
Abandoned vehicles must be removed from property
Yards must be free of debris and excessive storage

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Wallington requires dog licensing and enforces animal control regulations.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Excessive barking subject to complaints and fines
No exotic animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is regulated on borough streets to ensure access and safety.
keyRules: Overnight parking restrictions on designated streets
Snow emergency parking bans — vehicles must be off-street
No commercial vehicle parking on residential streets overnight
Time limits in the Paterson Avenue business district
Handicapped parking violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with housing codes and safety standards.
keyRules: Rental units must be registered with the Borough
Inspections required at change of tenancy
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Fire safety requirements for multi-family dwellings
Certificate of Occupancy required for rental units

other.title: Two-Family Home Regulations
other.summary: Wallington has specific regulations for its many two-family properties.
keyRules: Two-family homes must comply with all building and fire codes
Separate utility metering may be required
Parking requirements must be met on the property
Owner-occupied two-family homes subject to different rules than investment properties

publicSchools.name: Wallington Junior/Senior High School
publicSchools.notes: Combined junior and senior high school serving all Wallington students in grades 7-12

publicSchools.name: Frank W. Gavlak Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving all Wallington students through grade 6

privateSchools.name: Most Sacred Heart of Jesus School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic parish school with strong ties to the Polish-American community

whyMoveHere: Among the most affordable communities in Bergen County
Rich cultural heritage with authentic Polish bakeries and restaurants
Walkable commercial district along Paterson Avenue
Central location with easy highway access
Strong sense of community and active civic organizations

commuteToNYC.duration: 35-55 minutes to Port Authority Bus Terminal
commuteToNYC.notes: Wallington residents commute to NYC primarily via NJ Transit bus service along Paterson Avenue and Main Avenue, with routes to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Midtown Manhattan. The commute is approximately 35-55 minutes depending on traffic. Driving to the Secaucus Junction NJ Transit station is also an option.

costOfLiving.summary: Wallington is one of Bergen County's most affordable communities, making it attractive for first-time buyers, young families, and those seeking value in a convenient location.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 10% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Very affordable housing by Bergen County standards
Authentic cultural dining and shopping (especially Polish)
Central location with good highway access
Strong community bonds and neighborhood pride
cons: No NJ Transit rail service — bus-only commute
School ratings below Bergen County averages
Some traffic congestion on Paterson Avenue

restaurants.name: Polka Deli & Bakery
restaurants.description: Authentic Polish delicatessen and bakery offering fresh kielbasa, pierogies, smoked meats, and traditional pastries.

restaurants.name: Krakus Market & Deli
restaurants.description: Polish market and deli known for imported European goods, homemade sausages, and prepared Polish dishes.

restaurants.name: Andrea Salumeria
restaurants.description: Italian deli and grocery offering fresh mozzarella, prepared foods, imported Italian meats, and gourmet sandwiches.

restaurants.name: Red Star Tavern
restaurants.description: Neighborhood bar and restaurant serving American pub food in a casual, friendly atmosphere.

parks.name: Wallington Municipal Park
parks.description: Borough's main park featuring a playground, basketball courts, sports fields, and a community gathering area.

parks.name: Veteran's Field
parks.description: Sports field and memorial area used for youth athletics and community events.

parks.name: Passaic River Walkway (nearby)
parks.description: Walking paths along the Passaic River accessible from the southern end of Wallington.

events.name: Wallington Polish Festival
events.description: Annual celebration of the borough's Polish heritage featuring traditional food, music, dancing, and cultural performances.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual parade honoring veterans, marching through the borough's main streets.
events.when: Memorial Day, last Monday in May

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Community celebration with tree lighting, holiday music, and seasonal festivities.

attractions.name: Paterson Avenue Polish District
attractions.description: The heart of Wallington's Polish-American community, featuring authentic bakeries, delis, and shops that draw visitors from across the region.

attractions.name: Most Sacred Heart of Jesus Church
attractions.description: Historic Catholic parish church that has served as a spiritual and cultural center for Wallington's Polish-American community for generations.

shoppingAreas: Paterson Avenue — Main commercial street with Polish delis, bakeries, and shops
Passaic Park (nearby) — Shopping along the Passaic border
Garden State Plaza (Paramus) — Major shopping center a short drive away

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 24 Union Boulevard, Wallington, NJ 07057

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
